name: shortcode
summary: Register a WordPress shortcode via a Ready event listener.
description: Scaffolds a Ready listener that calls add_shortcode and renders output through a render() method.
problem: You want users to embed dynamic content in posts/pages via a shortcode tag without hand-wiring add_shortcode calls.
appliesWhen: The shortcode renders to HTML (forms, embeds, dynamic content)
Users will paste the shortcode into the classic editor or shortcode block
Attribute defaults can be expressed in PHP
avoidWhen: You're targeting block editor exclusively (write a Gutenberg block instead)
The output never changes per-render (use static HTML)
tradeoffs: One class plus a listener registration. Shortcode output is captured via output buffering — for raw string output, return directly from render() without ob_*.
outputs: A {name} class implementing CanHandle that registers the shortcode
A getListeners() entry mapping Ready to {name}
postApply: Replace the placeholder render output with the actual HTML

name.aiHint: PascalCase. Suffix with Shortcode for clarity.
